page: Amazon Web Services c7i.metal-24xl is a bare-metal, compute-optimized server powered by dedicated Intel Xeon 8488C processors. Built on the x86_64 architecture, it features 48 physical cores and 96 vCPUs running at a clock speed of 3.2 GHz. The processor configuration includes a 105 MB L3 cache, 2 MB L2 cache, and 48 KB L1d cache. The server is equipped with 192.0 GB of system memory, yielding a resource density of 2.0 GB per core. It does not include local storage or graphics processing units, but it provides a baseline network bandwidth of 38 Gbps.

Benchmark results show strong multi-core performance, with Geekbench multi-core and Passmark CPU scores ranking in the top tier (top 10%). The hardware excels in specialized instruction sets, achieving top-tier results in Passmark CPU extended instructions and encryption tests, aided by hardware-level support for Advanced Vector Extensions (AVX-512) and Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX). Memory performance is highly efficient, with cached small-block read-only memory bandwidth reaching the top 10% tier at 7016.02 GB/sec. Software compilation with Clang and text processing also rank in the top tier.

Given its high compute density and lack of bundled local storage, this bare-metal instance is optimized for compute-heavy workloads that do not require local NVMe drives. It is highly suitable for software compilation, high-throughput web serving, cryptography, and CPU-based machine learning inference, as demonstrated by strong performance in medium and large LLM prompt processing benchmarks. The 1:2 vCPU-to-RAM ratio makes it less suitable for memory-bound database workloads requiring massive RAM footprints, but highly cost-efficient for raw parallel processing tasks.
description: Amazon Web Services c7i.metal-24xl is a bare-metal, compute-optimized server featuring 96 vCPUs and 48 physical cores powered by dedicated Intel Xeon 8488C processors running at 3.2 GHz. It is equipped with 192.0 GB of RAM, providing a 1:2 vCPU-to-RAM ratio, and offers a baseline network bandwidth of 38 Gbps without local storage or GPUs. The server delivers top-tier performance in multi-core processing, software compilation, and cryptographic operations, supported by hardware-level AVX-512 and Intel AMX extensions. Benchmark data highlights exceptional cached memory bandwidth and strong performance in CPU-based LLM inference. This bare-metal configuration is highly cost-efficient for compute-intensive workloads, including parallel software compilation, high-throughput web serving, and cryptographic applications, though its lack of local storage requires external storage integration.
og_description: Amazon Web Services c7i.metal-24xl is a bare-metal, compute-optimized server featuring 96 vCPUs, 192 GB RAM, and dedicated Intel Xeon processors, delivering top-tier multi-core and cryptographic performance.

tagline: A bare-metal, compute-optimized server featuring ninety-six vCPUs and advanced matrix extensions for high-throughput parallel processing and cryptographic workloads.
bullet_points: Dedicated Intel Xeon 8488C processors with 96 vCPUs and 48 physical cores
192.0 GB of system memory providing a balanced 1:2 compute-to-RAM ratio
Top-tier multi-core performance, software compilation, and cryptographic execution
Hardware-accelerated AVX-512 and Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX)
Baseline network bandwidth of 38 Gbps with zero local storage or GPUs
Best suited for parallel software compilation, cryptography, and CPU-based LLM inference
